qa_pairs.question: What is the address for Graham's dedicated visualization nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham's dedicated visualization nodes are available at gra-vdi.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What type of connections do Graham's visualization nodes allow?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham's visualization nodes allow only VNC connections.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions on how to use Graham's visualization nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find instructions on how to use Graham's visualization nodes on the VNC page.

qa_pairs.question: Why was Graham's capacity reduced in early 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham's capacity was reduced in early 2025 to make space for the installation of the new Nibi cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Is Intel Turbo Boost enabled on Graham nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Turbo Boost is enabled for all Graham nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham have 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6248 Cascade Lake @ 2.5GHz CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA V100 Volta GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 2 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 2 nodes equipped with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6248 Cascade Lake CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA V100 Volta GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 2 nodes have 377G or 386048M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of storage do the 2 nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6248 Cascade Lake CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA V100 Volta GPUs have?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 5.0TB NVMe SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham are configured with 2 x Intel Xeon Silver 4110 Skylake @ 2.10GHz CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 6 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 6 nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Silver 4110 Skylake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 6 nodes have 187G or 191840M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What type of storage is found on the 6 nodes featuring 2 x Intel Xeon Silver 4110 Skylake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 11.0TB SATA SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham have 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake @ 2.10GHz CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 30 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 30 nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 30 nodes have 187G or 191840M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of storage do the 30 nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA T4 Turing GPUs have?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 5.8TB NVMe SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPU-only nodes on Graham have 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake @ 2.10GHz CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 136 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 136 CPU-only nodes equipped with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 136 nodes have 187G or 191840M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What storage capacity is available on the 136 CPU-only nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6238 Cascade Lake CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 879GB SATA SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham have 2 x AMD EPYC 7742 CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There is 1 such node on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the single node equipped with 2 x AMD EPYC 7742 CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: This node has 2000G or 2048000M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of storage does the single node with 2 x AMD EPYC 7742 CPUs and 8 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs have?
qa_pairs.answer: It has 3.5TB SATA SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham are configured with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6326 Cascade Lake @ 2.90GHz CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 2 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 2 nodes with 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6326 Cascade Lake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 2 nodes have 256G or 262144M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What type of storage is available on the 2 nodes featuring 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6326 Cascade Lake CPUs and 4 x NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 3.5TB SATA SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes on Graham have 1 x AMD EPYC 7713 CPU and 4 x NVIDIA RTX A5000 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 11 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 11 nodes with 1 x AMD EPYC 7713 CPU and 4 x NVIDIA RTX A5000 Ampere GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: These 11 nodes have 128G or 131072M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of storage do the 11 nodes with 1 x AMD EPYC 7713 CPU and 4 x NVIDIA RTX A5000 Ampere GPUs have?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 1.8TB SATA SSD storage.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPU-only nodes on Graham have 1 x AMD EPYC 7543 CPU?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 6 such nodes on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: What is the available memory on the 6 CPU-only nodes with 1 x AMD EPYC 7543 CPU?
qa_pairs.answer: These 6 nodes have 1024G or 1048576M of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What storage is available on the 6 CPU-only nodes with 1 x AMD EPYC 7543 CPU?
qa_pairs.answer: They have 8x2TB NVMe storage.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended practice for selecting node types for most applications on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended not to select a specific node type for most applications, as performance differences between Skylake and Cascade Lake nodes are expected to be small compared to job waiting times.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user constrain a CPU job to a Cascade Lake node on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: If it is necessary to constrain a CPU job, a user should use `--constraint=cascade`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the best practice for local on-node storage on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: The best practice for local on-node storage is to use the temporary directory generated by Slurm, referred to by the environment variable `$SLURM_TMPDIR`.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to the contents of the `$SLURM_TMPDIR` directory after a job completes on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: The `$SLURM_TMPDIR` directory and its contents will disappear upon job completion.

qa_pairs.question: Is the amount of available memory on Graham nodes exactly the 'round number' suggested by hardware configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the amount of available memory is less than the 'round number' suggested by hardware configuration because some is permanently occupied by the kernel and OS.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the scheduler on Graham not allocate jobs whose memory requirements exceed the specified available memory?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler will never allocate jobs whose memory requirements exceed the specified available memory to avoid wasting time by swapping/paging.

qa_pairs.question: Why might an I/O-intensive job benefit from requesting more memory than the aggregate size of its processes on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: An I/O-intensive job might benefit from requesting somewhat more memory because the memory allocated to the job must be sufficient for I/O buffering performed by the kernel and filesystem.

qa_pairs.question: What are the three generations of Tesla GPUs available on Graham, listed from oldest to newest?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham contains Tesla GPUs from three generations: V100 Volta, T4 Turing, and A100 Ampere.

qa_pairs.question: Which generation of GPUs has been decommissioned on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: P100 GPUs have been decommissioned on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: How does the V100 Volta GPU perform compared to its predecessor, the P100, for standard and deep learning computations?
qa_pairs.answer: The V100 Volta GPU offers about double the performance for standard computation and about 8X performance for deep learning computations utilizing its tensor core units compared to the P100.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key characteristics of the T4 Turing GPU on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: The T4 Turing GPU is targeted specifically at deep learning workloads, supports good single precision performance, has tensor cores, and supports reduced precision integer calculations, but it does not support efficient double precision computations.

qa_pairs.question: Are Pascal GPU nodes still available on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Pascal GPU nodes are no longer available on Graham.

qa_pairs.question: How many Volta nodes does Graham have?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham has a total of 2 Volta nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Do Volta nodes on Graham feature NVLINK interconnect?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Volta nodes on Graham have high bandwidth NVLINK interconnect.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum job duration for Volta nodes on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum job duration for Volta nodes on Graham is seven days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended CPU to GPU ratio for jobs on 28-core Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: On 28-core Volta nodes, the ratio of CPUs to GPUs should be kept at 3.5 or less.

qa_pairs.question: For a job using 4 GPUs on a 28-core Volta node, how many CPU cores should be requested at most?
qa_pairs.answer: For a job using 4 GPUs on a 28-core Volta node, at most 14 CPU cores should be requested.

qa_pairs.question: For a job using 1 GPU on a 28-core Volta node, what is the maximum number of CPU cores that should be requested?
qa_pairs.answer: For a job using 1 GPU on a 28-core Volta node, at most 3 CPU cores should be requested.

qa_pairs.question: Are users allowed to exceed the CPU to GPU ratio for short test jobs on Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users are allowed to run a few short test jobs (shorter than 1 hour) that break this rule to see how their code performs.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPU cores per GPU can be used on the two newest 40-core Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: On the two newest 40-core Volta nodes, users can use 5 CPU cores per GPU.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user specifically request one of the NVLINK Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should request an NVLINK Volta node directly by adding the `--constraint=cascade,v100` parameter to the job submission script.

qa_pairs.question: What is the benefit of using the fast local disk on Volta nodes for I/O-intensive jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: Using the fast local disk on Volta nodes, identified by the `$SLURM_TMPDIR` environment variable, is recommended for I/O-intensive jobs due to its performance benefits.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to files copied to `$SLURM_TMPDIR` on Volta nodes once a job finishes?
qa_pairs.answer: All files in `$SLURM_TMPDIR` will be removed once the job ends, so users do not have to clean up that directory themselves.

qa_pairs.question: Can Python virtual environments be created in the temporary space on Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Python virtual environments can be created in the temporary space specified by `$SLURM_TMPDIR` for greater efficiency.

qa_pairs.question: What parameter should be used to request two T4 Turing GPUs per node on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: To request two T4 Turing GPUs per node, `--gres=gpu:t4:2` should be specified in the job script.

qa_pairs.question: What parameters should be used to request two Ampere GPUs per node on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: To request two Ampere GPUs per node, `--gres=gpu:a100:2` or `--gres=gpu:a5000:2` should be specified in the job script.
